paper_identity.title: Efficient and Robust Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search Using Hierarchical Navigable Small World Graphs
paper_identity.venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

scientific_problem.problem: Power-law scaling complexity and global connectivity loss during routing in proximity graphs.
scientific_problem.motivation: Sustained volume growth of big data requires sub-linear, highly scalable similarity search approaches.
scientific_problem.gap: Traditional proximity graphs experience logarithmic degree inflation along the greedy search path, driving polylogarithmic complexity scaling.
scientific_problem.hypothesis: Explicit link separation via multi-layer scaling paired with a diversity-driven heuristic achieves true logarithmic scaling.
scientific_problem.research_question: Can general metric spaces be indexed purely using proximity graphs to yield logarithmic query scaling without auxiliary structures?
scientific_problem.success_criteria: Outperforming tree-based, LSH, and quantization baselines across heterogeneous datasets while remaining robust against highly clustered geometries.

concepts.name: Probabilistic Skip List
concepts.role: 1D foundational structure guiding the multi-layer hierarchy abstraction.

concepts.name: Relative Neighborhood Graph
concepts.role: Topological subgraph targeted by the edge selection heuristic to guarantee global cluster connectivity.

methods.name: SELECT-NEIGHBORS-HEURISTIC (Algorithm 4)
methods.role: Pruning and selecting graph edges based on geometric diversity rather than absolute proximity.
methods.input: Base element q, candidates C, number of links M, layer level l
methods.claimed_advantage: Maintains cluster boundary connectivity, enhancing recall on non-uniform data.

metrics.name: Recall
metrics.definition: Ratio of true nearest neighbors found over K requested.

baselines.name: Facebook Faiss
baselines.fairness: Fair, configured using high-performance open-source parameters.

claims.claim: HNSW search scales logarithmically O(log N) with data size.
claims.evidence: Benchmarks on controlled hypercube synthetic datasets up to 30M entries.
claims.risk: Dimensionality inflation causes scaling degradation due to Delaunay graph convergence to complete graphs.

limitations.limitation: Loss of native peer-to-peer distribution mechanics due to top-layer access congestion.
limitations.evidence: Stated explicitly in the discussion section.

research_gaps.gap: Lack of precise, non-degrading dynamic element deletion/removal mechanics.
research_gaps.opportunity: Developing local graph healing repair routines for HNSW layers.
